mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250k signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signals a strengthening consumer market that increases equity demand and prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% and a 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a strengthening consumer market and labor market that increases equity demand and prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income and falling unemployment rate are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 3-month decline in the housing market index signals a weakening supply chain and housing market that reduces corporate profitability and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production and housing market are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% and a 3-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures above 3% signals a strengthening consumer market that increases equity demand and prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable personal income and real personal consumption expenditures are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
